summary: Completed both backend and frontend e2e testing for Midasis Technologies website. Backend: 15/15 pytest passed (root, contact POST happy path for all 4 requirement_types, invalid email, invalid enum, all 5 missing-field cases, empty-string rejection, GET list + persistence). Frontend: home page, navbar, hero (with 'Where Technology Meets Talent' tagline + animated SVG globe + trust bar), all 11 sections present (about, us-staffing, india-staffing, why-choose, services, vision, clients, contact, footer), 4 stat cards, contact form fields and 3 office cards, smooth-scroll nav, mobile hamburger sheet with all 7 nav links, contact form submission end-to-end (success toast + form clear + MongoDB persistence verified via /api/contact GET), empty-form validation toast, vision section solid blue bg with white text, footer dark navy with Quick Links/Staffing/Reach Us. No critical or minor issues found.
critical_code_review_comments: Minor: in /app/backend/server.py the `logger` is referenced inside submit_contact (line 105) before being defined at line 133. Works at runtime because logger is only accessed when an exception fires, but should be defined near the top for clarity.
Minor: backend uses @app.on_event('shutdown') which is deprecated in newer FastAPI; consider migrating to lifespan context manager.
Acceptable: contact form relies on HTML5 `required` attribute + JS check; both paths work.
test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Created multiple TEST_-prefixed contact submissions during testing (visible via GET /api/contact). Not cleaned up, but they only populate test_database.contact_submissions and don't affect production behavior.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 22 frontend smoke checks + 15 pytest backend cases pass against https://staffing-hub-55.preview.emergentagent.com. Mobile menu uses [data-testid='mobile-menu-trigger'] and opens a Sheet with [role='dialog']. Select dropdown items have testids like contact-requirement-us-it-staffing. End-to-end contact submission verified persistence in MongoDB.
